a:r.a:t: These eight changes are about teaching agents to think, not just act. Reasoning Protocol is the highest-impact — it makes agents list out the steps before jumping to tool calls, which cuts the "tool spam" problem by a huge amount. Confidence Scoring extends what the RAG agent already does to all agents — score your certainty and flag when you're guessing. Self-Reflection Checkpoints mirror what expert humans do: after three tool calls, pause and reassess. Tool Selection Justification adds explicit reasoning about why you picked that specific tool over alternatives. Error Recovery Playbook unifies failure handling across all agents instead of eleven different approaches. Memory Trust Framework adds freshness awareness — old memories get flagged. Output Format Contract ensures responses match the task type. Delegation Context makes worker agents understand they're part of a delegated workflow. You can add these as new sections in each agent's markdown file, or inject them as a new reasoning block in the prompt-building function.
